violations.text: The double doors at one of the store entrances are not tight-fitting. Space observed at the bottom between the doors. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 08/11/2025

The doors to the outside, located in the store entrance, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. Work order has been placed. New Violation. Correct By: 05/06/2025

Molluscan Shellfish located in seafood display case did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. Tags were located and put with shellstock at the time of inspection. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

Turkey breast, which was prepared in advance, was not cooled to 70 degrees Fahrenheit in 2 hours and then to 41 degrees Fahrenheit within 4 hours. Per PIC, turkey breast was cooked 2 days prior to inspection and was observed at 48 DF. Turkey breast was voluntarily disposed of at the time of inspection. Proper cooling methods reviewed with PIC. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

The presence of mice is not controlled as evidenced by droppings in the bread, crouton, candy, and pet isle. The presence of insects, rodents, and other pests must be controlled by routinely inspecting the premises for evidence of pests, using acceptable methods to control pests, eliminating harborage conditions, and routinely inspecting incoming shipments of food and supplies. Establishment is to clean any and all droppings in all isles using appropriate methods to clean and sanitize to monitor new activity. Establishment is to increase pest control as frequently as needed and have a pest control report for next inspection. Report is to contain the frequency of treatment, types of bait, traps, initial kill numbers, structural findings, contributing factors, and technician assessment. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

The door to the outside, located in the front of the store, is not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 04/25/2025
The bakery walk-in cooler gasket was observed in poor repair. Gaskets must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/05/2025

The non-food contact surfaces of the bakery walk-in freezer fans, and the produce case have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. Non-food contact surfaces must be cleaned to sight and touch. New Violation. Correct By: 04/21/2025

The wall in the produce walk-in area is in poor repair. The ceiling tiles in the meat department area are in poor repair. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/21/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (knives) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/22/2024

The doors to the outside, located in the receiving areas, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
Deli case door was not in good repair. Equipment must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
The non-food contact surfaces of the perimeter ventilation grates have an accumulation of dust, dirt, food residue, and other debris. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
The ceiling in the bakery area (above the 3-door reach-in) is in poor repair. A tile is missing. The physical facilities must be maintained in good repair. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2024
